projectDescription: Open-source firmware platform for building game controller adapters and custom controllers on RP2040, ESP32-S3, and nRF52840 microcontrollers.
keyFeatures: Universal controller protocol translation (USB HID, XInput, Bluetooth, WiFi, native)
Support for retro consoles: GameCube, PCEngine, Dreamcast, Nuon, 3DO, N64, and more
Button remapping profiles with in-field switching via SELECT + D-pad
Dual-core execution: Core 0 for input, Core 1 for timing-critical console protocols
PIO-based cycle-accurate timing for console output protocols
Custom controller support via GPIO and ADC inputs
Assistive gaming: merge multiple input devices into one output
Low-latency zero-copy router with lock-free reads
publicApiSurface: router_submit_input(input_event_t*) — submit normalized controller input to router
router_get_output(output_target, slot) — read latest state for an output slot
InputInterface — interface for implementing new input sources
OutputInterface — interface for implementing new output targets
input_event_t — unified input event structure (buttons bitmap + analog axes)
JP_BUTTON_* — button constants in W3C Gamepad API order
profile_t — button remapping profile table structure
make <app>_<board> — build system entry point for firmware targets
